prose.title: 88m ait graduation requirements
prose.body:
To graduate from 88M Advanced Individual Training (AIT), there are certain requirements that need to be met. Here are some general graduation requirements for 88M AIT:

1. Physical Fitness: You must meet the Army's physical fitness standards, which typically include a certain score on the Army Physical Fitness Test (APFT) and/or passing the Army Combat Fitness Test (ACFT).

2. Completion of Training: You need to successfully complete all required training modules and pass any examinations or assessments associated with those modules. This includes both classroom instruction and hands-on training.

3. Driver Certification: As an 88M (Motor Transport Operator), you will need to earn a commercial driver's license (CDL) during your AIT. This involves passing both the written and practical CDL exams.

4. Safety Training: You will be required to complete various safety training courses, such as hazardous material handling and defensive driving.

5. Weapons Qualification: Depending on your unit assignment, you may need to qualify on various military weapons as part of your training. This will typically involve both classroom instruction and range qualification.

6. Conduct: You should maintain a satisfactory level of conduct during your time at AIT. This means following all rules, regulations, and standards of behavior set by your instructors and the Army.

Please keep in mind that specific graduation requirements and standards may vary based on changes in Army policies and AIT curriculum. It is always best to consult your training course and instructor for the exact requirements applicable to your situation.
